data.title: Why Bitcoin Is Sliding Toward $90K amid Trump–Greenland Tensions
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is sliding toward $90,000 as investors grow cautious amid rising geopolitical tensions. Comments involving U.S. President Donald Trump and Greenland have weighed on market sentiment, leading some traders to reduce exposure to riskier assets like cryptocurrencies. BTC is down 2% in today's trading, extending its losses to over 5% in the past five days.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's Driving the Plunge?

Over the weekend, Trump warned he would impose a 10% tariff on imports from Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Finland, and the UK starting February 1, with the rate rising to 25% on June 1 unless an agreement is reached on his proposal to bring Greenland under U.S. control.

Trump said on Monday that he plans to discuss Greenland during this week's World Economic Forum in Davos. While he did not name who he would meet, leaders from several European countries are expected to attend.

This led to negative sentiment around cryptocurrencies. Bitcoin dropped sharply, sliding from nearly $96,000 to just over $90,000 in the last few days. The second-largest cryptocurrency, Ethereum (ETH-USD), is also down nearly 6% over the past five days. Meanwhile, gold hit a fresh all-time high as investors rushed toward safe havens.

Bitcoin Volatile as Bank of Japan Rate Decision Looms

Investors also became nervous and sold Bitcoin ahead of the Bank of Japan's upcoming interest rate decision.

The central bank signaled it could raise interest rates if the economy and inflation stay strong, which pushed Japanese bond yields sharply higher. Rising bond yields make safer investments more attractive, causing investors to move money away from risky assets like Bitcoin.

Year-to-date, BTC is up by 5.54%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: UAL Earnings: United Airlines Post Mixed Financial Results, Forecasts Record Year Ahead
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) has reported mixed financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025, but said it could generate record earnings this year as travel demand strengthens.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Chicago-based airlines reported earnings per share (EPS) of $3.10, which was ahead of the $2.94 expected among analysts. Revenue in the October through December period totaled $15.40 billion, which matched the consensus forecast on Wall Street.

Management said the Q4 results were fueled by increasing demand for air travel, with sales rising for both premium seats and no-frills airline tickets. Looking ahead, the carrier said that it expects earnings per share of $12 to $14 in 2026, which is in line with the $13.16 consensus view of analysts.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Erase YTD Gains as Trump Intensifies Pressure on Greenland
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Tuesday, with both ETFs now in negative territory since the beginning of the year.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

President Trump hasn't backed down from his goal of acquiring Greenland and stressed the importance of the self-governing territory for U.S. national security. "As I expressed to everyone, very plainly, Greenland is imperative for National and World Security," he said in a Truth Social post. "There can be no going back — On that, everyone agrees!" Last week, he announced 10% tariffs on eight European countries that will remain in effect until the U.S. inks a deal to purchase Greenland.

Separately, Trump threatened to impose 200% tariffs on French champagne and wine after French President Emmanuel Macron expressed unwillingness to join the "Board of Peace" for Gaza.

Leaders across the European Union are set to meet on Thursday to discuss countermeasures against the tariffs, with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen promising a "unflinching, united and proportional" response. The bloc is considering a $108 billion tariff package on U.S. goods that was shelved last year after the two sides reached a trade deal. Another option is the anti-coercion instrument, which authorizes the EU to impose retaliatory tariffs, tax foreign companies, and limit market access in response to economic or political coercion from other countries.

Heightened geopolitical tensions have benefited safe-haven assets, such as gold (XAUUSD) and silver (XAGUSD). Both precious metals secured new all-time highs amid a weakening U.S. dollar and falling long-term Treasury prices.

However, selling U.S. assets is "quite a dangerous bet," according to UBS (UBS) CEO Sergio Ermotti. "Diversifying away from America is impossible," Ermotti said in an interview with Bloomberg Television at the World Economic Forum (WEF) in Davos, Switzerland. "The U.S. is the strongest economy in the world."

Looking ahead, Trump is set to meet with global leaders on Wednesday at the WEF and teased that he will deliver "a lot of messages." On Truth Social, he shared a screenshot of a conversation between himself and Macron that showed an invitation to a meeting with G7 leaders and representatives from Ukraine, Denmark, Syria, and Russia.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 2.06% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 2.12%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Are Cars About to Get Smaller? Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slides on the Idea
data.description:
Sometimes it seems like things combine in a fashion that suggests future developments. Legacy automaker Ford (F) is working on narrowing the affordability gap in cars. President Trump, meanwhile, is frantically advocating for small cars. Between the two, it suggests a certain path forward, but one Ford investors were not particularly pleased with. Ford shares slid nearly 3% in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trump recently noted that, perhaps, Ford might find something of a market in producing smaller cars, particularly those similar to the Japanese "kei" car. This would line up nicely with Ford's own plans to make cars more affordable. After all, a smaller car is generally cheaper to make than its larger equivalent, and a cheaper car can be sold for less money.

In fact, both CEO Jim Farley and Ford's Executive Chair Bill Ford are on the same page, planning to make vehicle affordability a top priority in 2026. This does not exactly line up with Ford's previously stated mission of "no boring cars," but a small car does not necessarily need to be a boring car. Ford revealed that the company has some plans in reserve to bring out more affordable cars, but has not announced these plans yet.

The Red Bull F1 Turbo Case

Another, more hair-raising, story recently emerged about a Ford Racing property that was nearly seized at customs. Specifically, it was a turbocharger that would have been part of a Formula 1 racer. The United Kingdom facility that built it had no place to test it, so they shipped it to the United States to let Ford handle the job. But the turbocharger ended up stuck in customs limbo, and Ford Racing had to get it out.

It turned out that the problem was a lack of correct forms, specifically, a manifest that answered certain questions about the turbocharger. In some cases, the customs operations are deferred as the parts in question would leave the country not long after arrival. But in this case, the necessary forms were not filed, and Ford had to move quickly to get the part out of customs' hands and onto Ford's test tracks.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 30.39% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.75 per share implies 3.62% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: NVDA, PLTR, GOOGL, NBIS, and Others: 5-Star Analyst Dan Ives Sees Greenland Tariff Noise as Chance to Own Big Tech Stocks
data.description:
Market volatility tied to the Greenland tariff dispute could weigh on stocks in the near term, but Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives said the pullback should be used to buy leading tech names such as Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Palantir (PLTR), Alphabet (GOOGL), CrowdStrike (CRWD), Nebius (NBIS), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), and Tesla (TSLA).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ives said markets are likely to open weaker as investors react to rising U.S.-Europe trade tensions, with President Donald Trump focusing on Greenland. The firm expects a short-term "risk-off" move, especially in AI-linked stocks.

Short-Term Pressure with Long-Term Opportunity

Ives said the current tariff "war of words" is weighing on sentiment and making investors more cautious, particularly toward technology stocks. However, he stressed that this type of trade noise has surfaced many times before and has not changed the market's broader direction.

Speaking from Davos, Ives noted that trade tensions are a key topic ahead of Trump's meetings with global leaders and tech executives at the World Economic Forum. Still, Wedbush expects talks and negotiations to ease tensions over time.

The firm's view is that "the bark will be worse than the bite," with negotiations likely to cool tensions over time, much as they have in past tariff disputes between the U.S. and Europe.

Why Wedbush Stays Bullish on Tech

Wedbush expects technology stocks to come under pressure first if markets turn cautious, but sees that weakness as a buying opportunity. The firm believes the AI cycle remains intact and expects a strong fourth-quarter earnings season, supported by roughly $550 billion in capital spending from Big Tech this year.

Ives said the U.S. is now clearly ahead of China in the global tech race, driven by heavy AI investment and innovation across major platforms and chipmakers.

He added that the Greenland tariff noise does not change the long-term outlook. Instead, Wedbush would use any pullback to add to core AI and tech leaders, as it sees the next phase of growth carrying into 2026.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which tech stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.

data.title: Selling U.S. Assets is a 'Dangerous Bet,' Warns UBS CEO
data.description:
Long-term U.S. Treasuries, stocks, and the dollar are all falling on Tuesday, although UBS (UBS) CEO Sergio Ermotti has cautioned that selling American assets is "quite a dangerous bet."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Diversifying away from America is impossible," Ermotti said in an interview with Bloomberg Television at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland. "The US is the strongest economy in the world."

UBS Sees Strong U.S. Potential amid Tariff Tensions

Ermotti added that both innovation and wealth continue to grow rapidly in the country, which have factored into UBS's planned wealth-management expansion in the U.S.

U.S. assets have faced pressure in recent days after President Trump announced tariffs on eight European countries that will remain in place until the U.S. secures a deal to acquire Greenland. The European Union is currently considering countermeasures, including a $108 billion tariff package on U.S. goods.

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Invests $150 Million in AI Firm Baseten
data.description:
Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has invested $150 million in Baseten, a start-up company that's focused on providing inference for artificial intelligence (AI) applications.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia's investment was part of a funding round that generated $300 million for Baseten at a $5 billion valuation. The latest funding round was led by IVP, a technology-focused venture capital firm, and CapitalG, which is Google parent company Alphabet's (GOOGL) venture fund.

Baseten last completed a funding raise of $150 million in September 2025. "As model-driven products become ubiquitous, we will be the invisible infrastructure behind the AI-first economy," said Baseten CEO Tuhin Srivastava in a news release.

AI Inference Explained

AI inference is the process where an artificial intelligence model applies what it has learned during training to real-world tasks such as identifying objects, translating languages, and answering questions. The AI model takes the knowledge it has learned and uses it to analyze data and make predictions, decisions, or generate outputs.

Baseten recently announced that it is offering AI companies text-to-video inferencing in the U.S., Finland and France. Nvidia and Baseten already have a relationship. Baseten provides support for Nvidia's Nemotron 3 Nano, a highly accurate, high-efficiency AI model.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 41 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $263.44 implies 46.91% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Elon Musk's xAI Is Testing AI 'Employees,' but the Results Are Sometimes Awkward
data.description:
Elon Musk's AI firm, xAI, is trying to figure out how to automate white-collar workers. In a recent podcast, a former engineer explained that xAI has been testing "human emulators," which are AI systems designed to behave like real employees. These virtual workers are already being used internally and are listed on org charts just like humans. In some cases, real employees did not even realize they were interacting with AI.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, the engineer, Sulaiman Ghori, said that these AI workers can sometimes cause confusion due to hallucinations or odd behaviors. For example, an AI might ask a coworker to come talk in person, only for that person to arrive at an empty desk. Notably, Ghori worked on xAI's Macrohard team, which Elon Musk has described as an effort to build AI that can automatically create software. The long-term goal is to challenge traditional enterprise tools from companies like Microsoft (MSFT), but Ghori made it clear that much of the work today is still manual and customized on a case-by-case basis.

In addition, he explained that building virtual employees for customers is difficult because people often forget to describe all the small steps involved in their jobs. Therefore, to improve results, xAI is considering running as many as one million human emulators at once, potentially by using spare computing power from Tesla (TSLA) vehicles while they are charging.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla. Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $397.47 per share implies 6% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: OpenAI Implements Age Verification System to Help Protect Children
data.description:
Privately held artificial intelligence (AI) start-up company OpenAI has introduced a new age verification system it says is designed to protect children.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Going forward, OpenAI's ChatGPT platform will identify users under the age of 18 and apply appropriate safeguards, said the company led by CEO Sam Altman. The new system examines behavioral patterns and account information, including age, typical usage times, and the user's stated age to determine if an account likely belongs to someone under age 18.

When the system identifies a potential minor, ChatGPT will automatically apply additional protections designed to limit exposure to sensitive content such as violence, sexual behavior, depictions of self-harm, and other content that is deemed to be extreme or potentially harmful. OpenAI is launching the age verification system amid growing calls for technology companies to do more to protect youth.

The Role of Parents

OpenAI says that parents can customize their child and teen's experience on ChatGPT through parental controls that include the ability to set time limits, implementing quiet hours, and receiving notifications if signs of acute distress are detected among minors using the AI chatbot.

OpenAI says that the protections have been developed using expert input and academic literature on child development. The company adds that it is working with organizations including the American Psychological Association and Global Physicians Network as it develops youth safety measures.

OpenAI has received more than $13 billion of investments from technology giant Microsoft (MSFT).

Is MSFT Stock a Buy?

Microsoft's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 34 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 32 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average price target on MSFT stock of $631.36 implies 37.29% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Gold Vaults to Record High as Greenland Crisis Intensifies
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is off to the races again on Tuesday, surging to a new all-time high above $4,700 per troy ounce and showing no signs of slowing down.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

President Trump hasn't dialed down his interest in acquiring Greenland and has reiterated the self-governing territory's importance to U.S. and global security. That provided a boost to gold, as the precious metal is viewed as a safe-haven asset that tends to perform well during periods of geopolitical uncertainty.

Investors Turn to Gold as Debasement Trade Heats Up, Dollar Weakens

Rising tensions have led to renewed interest in the "debasement trade," which is the act of shifting capital into hard assets, like gold, silver (XAGUSD), and real estate, from fiat currencies. "The debasement trade is on fire and precious metals are the outlet," said Robin Brooks, a senior fellow at the Brookings Institution.

A falling U.S. dollar is also benefiting gold, as it makes the metal cheaper for holders of other currencies, encouraging demand and supporting higher prices.
